info.name: KRYPSOS Super Agent Platform
info.description:
Complete API collection for KRYPSOS Super Agent AI Platform.
Base URL: https://super-agent-platform.onrender.com/api/v1/

Setup:
1. Set the 'base_url' variable to your Render URL
2. Call POST {{base_url}}/auth/login/ — token is auto-saved to the 'token' variable
3. All authenticated requests use Bearer {{token}} automatically

WebSockets (3 channels):
  wss://.../ws/tasks/<task_id>/        — live task step stream
  wss://.../ws/notifications/          — personal notification push
  wss://.../ws/agents/<agent_id>/live/ — agent live activity stream
All channels: Authorization: Bearer {{token}} header required
Keep-alive: send {"type":"ping"} → server replies {"type":"pong"}

Typical Flow:
1. GET /agents/templates/ → pick a template, note its numeric ID (1–5)
2. GET /agents/templates/<id>/ → preview template detail before activating
3. POST /agents/templates/<id>/activate/ → save agent_id
4. GET /agents/<agent_id>/overview/ → see activated agent stats and recent runs
5. POST /tasks/create/ → pass agent_id, save task_id, connect WebSocket
6. If task status = waiting_approval → GET /approvals/pending/ → POST /approvals/{id}/decide/
7. GET /tasks/{task_id}/ to see final result

Template IDs: 1=Email, 2=Research, 3=Document, 4=Calendar, 5=Reporting
All costs are returned in EUR (×0.92 from USD stored in DB).

variable.description: Paste your access token here after login

request.description: Check if the server is alive. No auth required.
Login and get JWT tokens. Test script auto-saves token to collection variable.

Returns the 4 settings cards for the Settings screen.

connected_apps: count + list of active integrations
cost: total spent this month in USD
budget: limit, period, % used, status (ok/warning/critical)
team: member count + list with name, email, role, avatar
Returns all home screen data in one call: active agents, tasks today, pending approvals, cost this month, recent activity.
Returns all active agents in the workspace.
Returns all ready-made agent templates. Each template shows tools, description, icon, and whether it's already added to the workspace.

Full detail of a template before activating it.

Template IDs:
  1 — Email Agent
  2 — Research Agent
  3 — Document Agent
  4 — Calendar Agent
  5 — Reporting Agent

Returns: name, description, capabilities (what_it_does), tools, max_cost_eur, already_added flag, and activated_agent_id if already in workspace.

Activate a ready-made agent by its numeric ID.

Template IDs:
  1 — Email Agent
  2 — Research Agent
  3 — Document Agent
  4 — Calendar Agent
  5 — Reporting Agent

Returns 201 if created, 200 if already exists in workspace.
Agent ID is auto-saved to the agent_id variable.

Full Agent Detail screen data: stats (tasks_today, success_rate, total_cost_eur), capability bullets (what_it_does), tool chips, recent_runs (last 3 tasks with time_ago + cost_label), and action URLs.
Live Activity screen: running task with progress bar, queue, live log entries. Poll every 3s while agent is running.
Filterable audit log for a single agent.

POST /api/v1/agents/templates/<id>/sync/

Syncs all agents in your workspace that were created from this template to the latest version.
Fields synced: system_prompt, tools, llm_model, max_steps, max_cost_usd.
Fields NOT synced: name, description (user may have customised).

Template IDs: 1=Email, 2=Research, 3=Document, 4=Calendar, 5=Reporting

Returns: {synced, skipped, template, version}

When to use:
- After deploying code changes that update a template
- To force-refresh an agent that has stale config

POST /api/v1/agents/sync-all-templates/

Syncs ALL template-based agents in the workspace to their latest versions in one call.

Returns: {results: [{template, version, synced, skipped}], total_synced}

Tip: Call this once after every deploy to ensure all users' agents are up to date.
Alternatively agents auto-sync at task runtime — so this is just a manual override.

query.description: queued | running | waiting_approval | completed | failed | cancelled

request.description: Returns form options for the New Task screen.

Returns all tasks currently waiting for user approval (status = waiting_approval).
Each task includes approval_id so the frontend can navigate directly to the approval action.

Example response:
[
  {
    "id": "<task_uuid>",
    "status": "waiting_approval",
    "approval_id": "<approval_uuid>",
    "agent_name": "Email Agent",
    "prompt": "Send weekly report...",
    ...
  }
]

Use approval_id to call POST /approvals/{approval_id}/decide/

Returns full task detail including:

  progress_percent     — 0–100. Computed from steps_taken/max_steps. 100 only when completed.
  deliverables         — files produced by the task. [] for Email Agent. [{name, url, type}] for Document Agent.
  total_steps_estimate — set by Orchestrator when splitting across agents.
  approval_id          — UUID of the current pending approval when status=waiting_approval, null otherwise.

Example response (waiting_approval):
{
  "status": "waiting_approval",
  "approval_id": "<approval_uuid>",
  "progress_percent": 60,
  "deliverables": [],
  "total_steps_estimate": null
}

Use approval_id to call POST /approvals/{approval_id}/decide/

Returns all ReAct loop steps. Each step now includes generic fields that work for any agent:

  agent_name — which agent handled this step (Email Agent name for now; sub-agent name when Orchestrator is live)
  title      — short human-readable label e.g. 'Sending email', 'Searching the web'
  detail     — one-liner e.g. 'To harideepak@...', 'Query: AI news 2026'

Example step:
{
  "step_number": 3,
  "step_type": "tool_call",
  "agent_name": "Email Agent",
  "title": "Sending email",
  "detail": "To harideepak@krypsos.tech",
  "tool_name": "send_email"
}

Demonstrates prompt auto-enhancement.

Short/vague prompts (< 12 words) are automatically expanded before the task runs.

Examples:
  'read email from hostinger'
  → 'Read the latest email from hostinger. Summarise the subject, sender, date, and key message in plain English.'

  'check my calendar'
  → 'List my upcoming events for today and tomorrow. Show the title, time, location, and attendees for each.'

  'schedule a meeting'
  → 'Find a free 1-hour slot in my calendar for today or tomorrow and create a meeting with a Google Meet link.'

PATCH /api/v1/tasks/<task_id>/draft/

Edit the email content and/or add attachments BEFORE approving a YELLOW send_email action.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
THE FLOW (main use-case):
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
1. Create task: POST /tasks/create/
   body: { "prompt": "send email to hari@gmail.com saying this is testing" }

2. Agent prepares the email → task pauses (status: waiting_approval)

3. BEFORE approving → call this PATCH to edit content + add attachments
   Send multipart/form-data with any of:
     to       — change recipient
     subject  — change subject
     body     — change email body
     files    — attach PDF, JPG, PNG, DOCX from your device

4. Approve: POST /approvals/{{approval_id}}/decide/
   body: { "decision": "approved" }

5. Email sends with YOUR edited content + attachments. Done!

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
RESPONSE (approval mode):
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
{
  "mode":         "approval",
  "approval_id":  "<uuid>",
  "to":           "hari@gmail.com",
  "subject":      "Test",
  "body_preview": "This is testing...",
  "attachments":  ["invoice.pdf"],
  "updated":      true,
  "next_step":    "Approve the task to send the email with your changes."
}

If task used create_gmail_draft tool instead → updates the Gmail draft directly (mode: gmail_draft).

All fields are optional — only send what you want to change.

formdata.value:
Hi Hari,

This is a test email from the Super Agent Platform.

Best,
Deepak

formdata.description: Attach files: PDF, JPG, PNG, DOCX (optional, supports multiple)

Invite a user who already has an account. No email sent — they see it in-app via GET /team/invites/. Roles: owner, admin, member, viewer.

query.description: Pass 'true' to return only unread notifications

request.description:
Returns up to 50 notifications for the logged-in user, newest first.

Notification types:
  task_complete — agent finished a task
  task_failed — agent task failed or approval rejected
  approval_needed — agent is waiting for your approval
  approval_decided — your approval was approved or rejected by a reviewer
  team_invite — someone invited you to a workspace
  budget_alert — spending near the limit
  integration_error — connected app has an issue
Returns only unread notifications.
Get a single notification detail.
Mark a single notification as read. Call this when the user opens/taps a notification. Sets is_read=true and saves read_at timestamp. No body required.
Permanently delete a single notification.
Mark all notifications as read at once. Call when user opens the notifications screen.
Get the user's notification preferences.

body.raw:
{
  "email_on_task_complete": true,
  "email_on_task_failed": true,
  "email_on_approval_needed": true,
  "email_on_budget_alert": true,
  "push_enabled": true
}

request.description: Toggle which notification types the user receives. All fields are optional — send only what you want to change.
resource_type: agent | task | approval | integration

GET /api/v1/audit/task/<task_id>/timeline/

Returns the full task audit timeline for the Task Audit screen.

Response shape:
  task:
    id, prompt, status, start_time, end_time, duration, cost, agent_name, agent_type

  timeline[]: ordered list of events
    id           — unique event id
    actor        — who did this (user name or agent name)
    actor_type   — 'user' | 'agent' | 'system'
    icon         — icon name (user, mail, file-text, calendar, check-circle...)
    action       — short label (e.g. 'Created task', 'Reading emails')
    detail       — subtitle (e.g. 'via app', 'Fetching 3 emails', '8m 35s · €0.33')
    time         — HH:MM:SS timestamp
    status_label — created | started | thinking | running | done | wait | approved | rejected | complete | failed
    status_color — hex color for the badge

Events included:
  1. User created task
  2. Agent started
  3. Each tool call (reading emails, sending email, etc.)
  4. Approval wait + user decision (if YELLOW tool triggered)
  5. Task complete / failed

query.description: All words must appear — 'send email' returns results that contain BOTH 'send' AND 'email' as whole words

request.description:
Searches tasks (prompt, result, agent name), agents (name, type, description), and audit events simultaneously. Returns grouped results with count per group.

Whole-word AND matching: 'send email' finds entries containing both words. 'read' will NOT match 'ready' or 'already'.

query.description: All words must appear — 'send email' finds tasks containing BOTH 'send' AND 'email' as whole words in prompt/result/agent name/status

request.description:
Search tasks by keyword. Multi-word queries use AND — 'send email' finds tasks that contain both words.

Whole-word matching: 'read' matches 'Read my emails' but NOT 'Q2 report ready'.

query.description: All words must appear — 'email agent' finds agents whose name/type/description contain BOTH words

request.description:
Search agents by keyword. Multi-word queries use AND — 'email agent' finds agents that contain both words across name, agent_type, and description.

Whole-word matching: 'email' matches 'Email Agent' but NOT 'emailagent'.

Personal notification channel — one connection per logged-in user.

Auth: Add header  Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

Events received:
  connected    — handshake confirmed
  notification — new notification pushed, contains full notification object in 'data'

Notification types in data:
  task_complete     — agent finished a task
  task_failed       — agent task failed
  approval_needed   — agent needs your approval
  approval_decided  — your approval was approved or rejected
  team_invite       — someone invited you to a workspace

Keep-alive: send {"type": "ping"} → server replies {"type": "pong"}

Typical use: connect on app launch, update notification badge count in real-time without polling.

Streams live thought / action / observation events for a specific agent.

Auth: Add header  Authorization: Bearer {{token}}

Events received:
  connected    — handshake confirmed
  agent_event  — thought, tool call, or observation from the running agent

Keep-alive: send {"type": "ping"} → server replies {"type": "pong"}

Typical use: power the Agent Live Activity screen — show what the agent is thinking and doing right now.

After approval: Calendar event created + Google Meet link generated + invitations sent to attendees

If you only have names (not emails), say:
'Create a meeting at 11am with Arun and Sankar'
Agent will use search_customer_by_email to resolve names → emails from customer memory

Tests: current_time → search_customer_by_email → create_meeting (YELLOW)
Agent resolves 'Arun' and 'Sankar' to email addresses using customer memory
Then creates Calendar event with those emails as attendees

Will pause at waiting_approval
Safety: moves to Gmail trash, NOT permanent delete

GREEN — runs automatically

Approve via /approvals/{{approval_id}}/decide/

GREEN — runs automatically

item.name: 💾 Draft Reply + Save to Gmail Drafts [GREEN]

request.description:
Tests: read_emails → get_customer_memory → draft_reply → create_gmail_draft
All GREEN — no approval needed
Draft is saved to Gmail's Drafts folder (visible in Gmail app)
User can review and send from Gmail, or approve via agent

Tests: create_gmail_draft directly
GREEN — saves to Gmail Drafts without any approval
Check Gmail Drafts folder to see the saved draft

item.name: ✏️ Edit Email Before Approve + Add Attachment

request.description:
Edit email content + attach files BEFORE approving a send_email action.

FLOW:
1. POST /tasks/create/ → prompt: 'send email to hari@gmail.com saying testing'
2. Task pauses → status: waiting_approval
3. Call this PATCH to change body/subject/to and/or upload files
4. POST /approvals/{{approval_id}}/decide/ → { "decision": "approved" }
5. Email sends with your edits + attachments

All fields optional — send only what you want to change.

formdata.value:
Hi,

Please find the updated invoice attached.

Best regards,
Deepak

request.description:
Full flow: generate_content → create_pdf → upload_to_drive [YELLOW]
Task will pause at waiting_approval before Drive upload.
Approve via POST /api/v1/tasks/{task_id}/approve/{approval_id}/
Agent: Document Agent

Returns top 4 most-used prompts ranked by frequency from task history.

New user (no task history) gets these 4 defaults:
  1. Summarise unread emails
     → Read all my unread emails and give me a clear summary
  2. Download latest attachment
     → Find the most recent email with an attachment and download it
  3. Reply to an email
     → Read latest unread email, create a reply, and send it
  4. Summary of last 5 emails
     → Read my last 5 emails and summarise each one

Once user starts running tasks, defaults are replaced by their actual top prompts.

Response fields:
  id         — stable UUID from the prompt
  title      — short label (first 6 words)
  prompt     — full prompt to pre-fill on tap
  icon       — mail | paperclip | send | inbox | file-text | calendar | zap
  agent_type — email | calendar | document
  source     — 'usage' (from history) | 'default'
  run_count  — how many times this prompt was used

Hide a prompt permanently from the quick task list.
Pass the full prompt text.

The prompt will never appear again even if it's the most used.
Use this when user taps 'dismiss' or 'remove' on a quick task card.

GET /api/v1/profile/settings-summary/

Returns the 4 settings cards:

connected_apps:
  count: number of active integrations
  apps: ["gmail", "google_calendar", "google_drive"]

cost:
  this_month_usd: total cost this month
  currency: "USD"

budget:
  set: true/false
  limit_usd: 20.00
  period: "monthly"
  used_pct: 7
  status: "ok" / "warning" / "critical"

team:
  member_count: 4
  members: [{name, email, role, avatar_url}]
